scenarios.title: Taking the Bus
scenarios.description: Practice asking about routes, paying, and being polite on public transit.

scenarios.title: Picking Up Medicine
scenarios.description: Practice giving your name, asking questions, and understanding instructions.

scenarios.title: Returning Something to a Store
scenarios.description: Practice politely explaining a return, providing proof, and resolving the issue.

scenarios.title: Asking for Directions
scenarios.description: Practice politely asking where something is and understanding the response.

scenarios.title: Getting a Haircut
scenarios.description: Practice describing what you want at the barber or hair salon.

scenarios.title: Picking Up a Food Order
scenarios.description: Practice giving your name, confirming your order, and handling issues politely.

scenarios.title: Asking a Neighbor for Help
scenarios.description: Practice introducing yourself and making a polite request to a neighbor.

scenarios.title: Talking to Your Coach
scenarios.description: Practice asking about practice, accepting feedback, and communicating your needs.

scenarios.title: Ordering at a Coffee Shop
scenarios.description: Practice ordering a drink, customizing it, and handling payment politely.

scenarios.title: Introduction at Job Shadowing
scenarios.description: Practice professional greetings, small talk, and showing interest in a workplace.

bu_a1_bridge.say: Let us practice using public transportation.

bu_a1_q1.say: Why is it useful to know how to take the bus?

bu_a1_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Why is it useful to know how to take the bus?

bu_a1_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: Knowing how to take the bus helps me get around independently without needing a ride. Let's keep going.

bu_a1_q2.say: What should you know before getting on the bus?

bu_a1_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. What should you know before getting on the bus?

bu_a1_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: I should know the route number and where to board and get off. Let's keep going.

bu_a1_q3.say: How do you find out which bus to take?

bu_a1_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. How do you find out which bus to take?

bu_a1_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: I can use a transit app or ask someone to find the right route. Let's keep going.

bu_a1_q4.say: What should you bring when taking the bus?

bu_a1_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. What should you bring when taking the bus?

bu_a1_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: I should bring exact fare or a transit card and know my destination. Let's keep going.

bu_a1_q5.say: Ready to practice the bus conversation?

bu_a1_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Ready to practice the bus conversation?

bu_a1_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: Yes I am ready to practice taking the bus. Let's keep going.

bu_a2_bridge.say: At the bus stop — asking for information.

bu_a2_q1.say: You see someone waiting at the stop. What do you ask?

bu_a2_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You see someone waiting at the stop. What do you ask?

bu_a2_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: Excuse me do you know if this bus goes downtown. Let's keep going.

bu_a2_q2.say: They say yes route seven goes downtown. What do you say?

bu_a2_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They say yes route seven goes downtown. What do you say?

bu_a2_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: Thank you so much that is really helpful. Let's keep going.

bu_a2_q3.say: You check the schedule and the bus is late. What do you think?

bu_a2_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You check the schedule and the bus is late. What do you think?

bu_a2_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: It must be running a little late I will wait patiently and check again in a minute. Let's keep going.

bu_a2_q4.say: Someone asks you if the bus is late. What do you say?

bu_a2_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Someone asks you if the bus is late. What do you say?

bu_a2_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: Yes it seems to be running a bit late it should be here soon though. Let's keep going.

bu_a2_q5.say: The bus arrives. What do you do first?

bu_a2_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. The bus arrives. What do you do first?

bu_a2_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: I would step back and let passengers get off before I board. Let's keep going.

bu_a3_q1.say: You step up to board. The driver greets you. What do you say?

bu_a3_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You step up to board. The driver greets you. What do you say?

bu_a3_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: Hi good afternoon thank you. Let's keep going.

bu_a3_q2.say: The driver asks where you are going. What do you say?

bu_a3_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. The driver asks where you are going. What do you say?

bu_a3_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: I am going to the downtown stop near Main Street please. Let's keep going.

bu_a3_q3.say: You pay the fare. What do you say?

bu_a3_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You pay the fare. What do you say?

bu_a3_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: Here is my fare thank you. Let's keep going.

bu_a3_q4.say: They say take a seat. What do you say?

bu_a3_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They say take a seat. What do you say?

bu_a3_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: Thank you have a great day. Let's keep going.

bu_a3_q5.say: You find a seat. What should you keep in mind?

bu_a3_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You find a seat. What should you keep in mind?

bu_a3_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: I should keep my voice low respect others space and give up my seat if needed. Let's keep going.

bu_a4_bridge.say: Riding the bus and getting off.

bu_a4_q1.say: You are not sure when your stop is coming. What do you do?

bu_a4_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You are not sure when your stop is coming. What do you do?

bu_a4_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: I would check my phone map or watch the stop signs to know when to get off. Let's keep going.

bu_a4_q2.say: Your stop is next. What do you do?

bu_a4_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Your stop is next. What do you do?

bu_a4_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: I would press the stop button or ring the bell to signal my stop. Let's keep going.

bu_a4_q3.say: The driver announces your stop. What do you say as you exit?

bu_a4_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. The driver announces your stop. What do you say as you exit?

bu_a4_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: Thank you have a good day driver. Let's keep going.

bu_a4_q4.say: You get off and realize it was the wrong stop. What do you do?
bu_a4_q4.hint: Stay calm and figure it out

bu_a4_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You get off and realize it was the wrong stop. What do you do?
bu_a4_q4_r.hint: Stay calm and figure it out

bu_a4_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: I would stay calm check my map and find the next bus or walk if it is close. Let's keep going.

bu_a4_q5.say: You find the correct stop. How do you feel?

bu_a4_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You find the correct stop. How do you feel?

bu_a4_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: I feel relieved I managed to figure it out on my own. Let's keep going.

bu_a5_q1.say: You are not sure if you are on the right bus. What do you ask the driver?

bu_a5_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You are not sure if you are on the right bus. What do you ask the driver?

bu_a5_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: Excuse me does this bus stop at Main Street downtown. Let's keep going.

bu_a5_q2.say: Driver says yes three more stops. What do you say?

bu_a5_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Driver says yes three more stops. What do you say?

bu_a5_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: Thank you I will watch for my stop in three stops. Let's keep going.

bu_a5_q3.say: You realize you do not have enough fare. What do you say?

bu_a5_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You realize you do not have enough fare. What do you say?

bu_a5_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: I am sorry I am a little short on fare is there anything I can do. Let's keep going.

bu_a5_q4.say: Driver says they cannot accept partial fare. What do you do?

bu_a5_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Driver says they cannot accept partial fare. What do you do?

bu_a5_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: I understand I am sorry I will get off and sort it out thank you. Let's keep going.

bu_a5_q5.say: You find the right amount in your bag. What do you say?

bu_a5_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You find the right amount in your bag. What do you say?

bu_a5_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: I found the right amount here you go I am sorry for the confusion. Let's keep going.

bu_a6_q1.say: What should you do if someone bothers you on the bus?
bu_a6_q1.hint: Tell the driver or move seats

bu_a6_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. What should you do if someone bothers you on the bus?
bu_a6_q1_r.hint: Tell the driver or move seats

bu_a6_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: I would move to a different seat or quietly tell the driver if I felt unsafe. Let's keep going.

bu_a6_q2.say: Why should you give up your seat for elderly people?

bu_a6_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Why should you give up your seat for elderly people?

bu_a6_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: It is the respectful and kind thing to do elderly people need the seat more. Let's keep going.

bu_a6_q3.say: What should you do with your headphones on the bus?

bu_a6_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. What should you do with your headphones on the bus?

bu_a6_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: I should keep the volume low so my music does not disturb other passengers. Let's keep going.

bu_a6_q4.say: Someone asks you for directions on the bus. What do you say?

bu_a6_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Someone asks you for directions on the bus. What do you say?

bu_a6_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: I am not totally sure but I think it is a few stops ahead let me double check. Let's keep going.

bu_a6_q5.say: What do you do with your trash on the bus?

bu_a6_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. What do you do with your trash on the bus?

bu_a6_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: I would keep it with me and throw it away when I find a bin at the stop. Let's keep going.

bu_a7_bridge.say: Planning a bus trip in advance.

bu_a7_q1.say: You want to take the bus to a new place. What do you do first?

bu_a7_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You want to take the bus to a new place. What do you do first?

bu_a7_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: I would look up the route number and stop information before I leave. Let's keep going.

bu_a7_q2.say: You find the route but are not sure about the timing. What do you check?

bu_a7_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You find the route but are not sure about the timing. What do you check?

bu_a7_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: I would check the schedule to see when the bus leaves and how long it takes. Let's keep going.

bu_a7_q3.say: You want to know the fare. How do you find out?

bu_a7_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You want to know the fare. How do you find out?

bu_a7_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: I can check the transit website or app to find the current fare amount. Let's keep going.

bu_a7_q4.say: You ask a friend who has taken that route. What do you say?

bu_a7_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You ask a friend who has taken that route. What do you say?

bu_a7_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: Have you taken route seven before any tips on where to get on or off. Let's keep going.

bu_a7_q5.say: They give you advice. What do you say?

bu_a7_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They give you advice. What do you say?

bu_a7_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: Thank you that is really helpful I feel much more prepared now. Let's keep going.

bu_a8_q1.say: You arrive at the stop and are not sure it is the right one. What do you say to someone nearby?

bu_a8_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You arrive at the stop and are not sure it is the right one. What do you say to someone nearby?

bu_a8_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: Excuse me do you know if route seven stops here to go downtown. Let's keep going.

bu_a8_q2.say: They confirm it does. Bus arrives. What do you do first?

bu_a8_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They confirm it does. Bus arrives. What do you do first?

bu_a8_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: I would step back and wait for passengers to exit before boarding. Let's keep going.

bu_a8_q3.say: You board and greet the driver. What do you say?

bu_a8_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You board and greet the driver. What do you say?

bu_a8_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: Hi good afternoon how are you today. Let's keep going.

bu_a8_q4.say: You pay and the driver says take a seat. What do you say?

bu_a8_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You pay and the driver says take a seat. What do you say?

bu_a8_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: Thank you so much I appreciate it. Let's keep going.

bu_a8_q5.say: Your stop comes. You get off. What do you say to the driver?

bu_a8_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Your stop comes. You get off. What do you say to the driver?

bu_a8_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: Thank you driver have a great rest of your day goodbye. Let's keep going.

bu_win.say: Great job! You navigated the bus like a pro — asked the right questions, paid, and rode safely! You earned a star!

py_a1_bridge.say: Let us practice picking up medicine at the pharmacy.

py_a1_q1.say: Why might you need to go to a pharmacy?

py_a1_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Why might you need to go to a pharmacy?

py_a1_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: I would go to pick up a prescription my doctor sent in. Let's keep going.

py_a1_q2.say: What information do you need when picking up medicine?

py_a1_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. What information do you need when picking up medicine?

py_a1_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: I need my name my date of birth and sometimes the prescription number. Let's keep going.

py_a1_q3.say: What should you do if you have questions about the medicine?

py_a1_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. What should you do if you have questions about the medicine?

py_a1_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: I should ask the pharmacist any questions I have before leaving. Let's keep going.

py_a1_q4.hint: Say: a trained expert in medicines

py_a1_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. What is a pharmacist?
py_a1_q4_r.hint: Say: a trained expert in medicines

py_a1_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: A pharmacist is a trained expert who knows about medicines and can answer my questions. Let's keep going.

py_a1_q5.say: Ready to practice picking up medicine?

py_a1_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Ready to practice picking up medicine?

py_a1_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: Yes I am ready to practice picking up medicine. Let's keep going.

py_a2_q1.say: You walk up to the pharmacy counter. What do you say?

py_a2_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You walk up to the pharmacy counter. What do you say?

py_a2_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: Hi I am here to pick up a prescription please. Let's keep going.

py_a2_q2.say: They ask for your name. What do you say?

py_a2_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They ask for your name. What do you say?

py_a2_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: My name is Alex Smith please. Let's keep going.

py_a2_q3.say: They ask for your date of birth. What do you say?

py_a2_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They ask for your date of birth. What do you say?

py_a2_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: My date of birth is March 5 2010. Let's keep going.

py_a2_q4.say: They search and find your prescription. What do you say?

py_a2_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They search and find your prescription. What do you say?

py_a2_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: Great thank you for finding it. Let's keep going.

py_a2_q5.say: They say it will be ready in ten minutes. What do you say?

py_a2_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They say it will be ready in ten minutes. What do you say?

py_a2_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: Okay no problem I will wait over here thank you. Let's keep going.

py_a3_q1.say: They hand you the bag. What should you ask before leaving?

py_a3_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They hand you the bag. What should you ask before leaving?

py_a3_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: Could you walk me through how to take this and if there is anything I should know. Let's keep going.

py_a3_q2.say: They say take one pill twice a day with food. What do you say?

py_a3_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They say take one pill twice a day with food. What do you say?

py_a3_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: So that is one pill twice a day with food understood thank you. Let's keep going.

py_a3_q3.say: You ask: What are the side effects?

py_a3_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You ask: What are the side effects?

py_a3_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: Are there any common side effects I should watch out for. Let's keep going.

py_a3_q4.say: They mention possible drowsiness. What do you say?

py_a3_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They mention possible drowsiness. What do you say?

py_a3_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: Good to know I will be careful and avoid driving if I feel drowsy. Let's keep going.

py_a3_q5.say: You ask: What happens if I miss a dose?

py_a3_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You ask: What happens if I miss a dose?

py_a3_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: What should I do if I forget to take a dose. Let's keep going.

py_a4_q1.say: The label says take with water. What does that mean?
py_a4_q1.hint: Say: swallow with a glass of water

py_a4_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. The label says take with water. What does that mean?
py_a4_q1_r.hint: Say: swallow with a glass of water

py_a4_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: It means I should swallow the pill with a full glass of water. Let's keep going.

py_a4_q2.say: The label says avoid sunlight. What should you do?

py_a4_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. The label says avoid sunlight. What should you do?

py_a4_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: I should wear sunscreen and try to limit time in direct sunlight. Let's keep going.

py_a4_q3.say: The label says complete the full course. What does that mean?

py_a4_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. The label says complete the full course. What does that mean?

py_a4_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: It means I should take all of the pills even if I feel better before they are gone. Let's keep going.

py_a4_q4.say: You are not sure about a word on the label. What do you do?

py_a4_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You are not sure about a word on the label. What do you do?

py_a4_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: I would ask the pharmacist to explain the word I do not understand. Let's keep going.

py_a4_q5.say: The pharmacist explains it. How do you respond?

py_a4_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. The pharmacist explains it. How do you respond?

py_a4_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: Thank you now I understand much better I appreciate your help. Let's keep going.

py_a5_q1.say: They tell you the cost. What do you say?

py_a5_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They tell you the cost. What do you say?

py_a5_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: Okay here is my payment thank you. Let's keep going.

py_a5_q2.say: They ask if you have insurance. What do you say?

py_a5_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They ask if you have insurance. What do you say?

py_a5_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: Yes I have insurance here is my card. Let's keep going.

py_a5_q3.say: With insurance the cost is lower. What do you say?

py_a5_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. With insurance the cost is lower. What do you say?

py_a5_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: Great thank you insurance really helps with the cost. Let's keep going.

py_a5_q4.say: They hand you the receipt. What do you say?

py_a5_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They hand you the receipt. What do you say?

py_a5_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: Thank you so much and have a great day. Let's keep going.

py_a5_q5.say: As you leave the pharmacist says feel better soon. What do you say?

py_a5_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. As you leave the pharmacist says feel better soon. What do you say?

py_a5_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: Thank you I appreciate that I hope so too. Let's keep going.

py_a6_bridge.say: What if the prescription is not ready?

py_a6_q1.say: They say the prescription has not been sent yet. What do you do?

py_a6_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They say the prescription has not been sent yet. What do you do?

py_a6_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: I would call my doctor office to check if they sent it and when to expect it. Let's keep going.

py_a6_q2.say: You call the doctor office. What do you say?

py_a6_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You call the doctor office. What do you say?

py_a6_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: Hi I am calling because the pharmacy has not received my prescription could you check. Let's keep going.

py_a6_q3.say: They say it was sent ten minutes ago. What do you say?

py_a6_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They say it was sent ten minutes ago. What do you say?

py_a6_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: Thank you I will go back to the pharmacy and ask them to check again. Let's keep going.

py_a6_q4.say: At the pharmacy you explain. What do you say?

py_a6_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. At the pharmacy you explain. What do you say?

py_a6_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: The doctor office confirmed they sent it about ten minutes ago could you check again please. Let's keep going.

py_a6_q5.say: They find it and apologize. What do you say?

py_a6_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They find it and apologize. What do you say?

py_a6_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: No problem at all these things happen thank you for sorting it out. Let's keep going.

py_a7_q1.say: Two days into your medicine you feel worse. What do you do?

py_a7_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Two days into your medicine you feel worse. What do you do?

py_a7_q1_t.say: No problem! You could say: I would call my doctor or ask the pharmacist if my reaction is normal. Let's keep going.

py_a7_q2.say: You call the pharmacy. What do you say?

py_a7_q2_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You call the pharmacy. What do you say?

py_a7_q2_t.say: No problem! You could say: Hi I have been taking the medicine for two days and I am feeling worse is that normal. Let's keep going.

py_a7_q3.say: They say to stop and call the doctor. What do you say?

py_a7_q3_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They say to stop and call the doctor. What do you say?

py_a7_q3_t.say: No problem! You could say: Okay I will stop taking it and call my doctor right away thank you. Let's keep going.

py_a7_q4.say: Doctor adjusts the medicine. You pick up the new one. What do you ask?

py_a7_q4_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. Doctor adjusts the medicine. You pick up the new one. What do you ask?

py_a7_q4_t.say: No problem! You could say: How do I take this one and is it different from the last prescription. Let's keep going.

py_a7_q5.say: They explain. How do you respond?

py_a7_q5_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. They explain. How do you respond?

py_a7_q5_t.say: No problem! You could say: Thank you I understand and I will follow the new instructions carefully. Let's keep going.

py_a8_q1.say: You walk up to the counter. What do you say?

py_a8_q1_r.say: No worries! Give it another try. You walk up to the counter. What do you say?
